evidence.text: - in office policy is pretty relaxed most people are in 2-3 days per week on average in busy season it tends to go up and in less busy times people go in much less - firm benefits are amazing healthcare plans and 401k match are good time off is accrued but carries over yoy and paid out upon leaving if you have balance left - large start class if you begin out of college is great there are not many companies where yo...
great people usually good pay good insurance 12 free counseling sessions a year backup child pet care fewer for pets usually good hours depending on the project
good benefits good pay my teams have been nice lots of learning
- benefits such as gym pass free workphone etc - talented and friendly people - pay is solid
big 4 perk lots of resources good pay and benefits
- can have good work life balance offers remote work

great wfh policy totally flexible generally speaking nice cool people work there good work life balance when it is not a busy season a lot of time off when not busy season 2 firm wide shutdowns nice amount of happy hours and stuff at least in my group
work life balance is a suggestion
great work culture great ethics awesome benefits excellent work life balance overall there can be some projects that are very demanding but on an average a good work family life balance
- good work life balance - complete remote work
access to world class client base working on problems that affect entire industries solid secure career path hard work success are recognised and rewarded

high demand and strong job security competitive salary and benefits exposure to different industries and clients continuous learning and skill development opportunity to solve complex real-world problems flexible working options remote hybrid clear career growth with certifications and specialization

pros -flexible schedule hybrid schedule where the assurance team only needs to go to the office twice a week -for the internship it's mainly one big project shadow opportunities and networking chats -free training in florida which is really one giant social for interns -happy hours and promotion days are a bonus after work

pwc provides an exceptional environment for career development offering access to challenging high-impact projects that build both technical expertise and leadership skills the firm's commitment to professional growth is evident through its robust training programs and the opportunity to work with a diverse talented team
learned a lot and was given leadership roles

one of the main advantages of working here is the friendly and supportive team colleagues are always ready to help and share their experience which creates a positive and collaborative work environment another benefit is the availability of regular training sessions that help employees grow professionally and stay up to date with new skills

it is a good platform to grow and learn offering the opportunity to explore a vast sea of resources and opportunities the environment encourages continuous development with support systems in place to help navigate challenges it also provides a valuable chance to enhance teamwork and leadership skills through collaboration and real-world problem-solving
- median compensation - supportive work environment - promotional opportunities
- pay - benefits - teamwork collaboration - student loan paydown
i love the team environment
team are supportive with good culture havent met anyone that is toxic
job security great benefits flexible vacation
lots of opportunities management cares
ethical and good emphasis on client centric
be an intern there you will get paid well and they won't abuse you yet then get out before you become an associate and your hourly wage ends up being 12
medical benefits were not great meetings meetings meetings no real room to advance in my role
work life balance is not great

-seniors and managers often won't know what you're talking about since they're not in the weeds so you will have to be very self sufficient and often can't use them as a resource before they say did you look at last year did you email client etc -more incompetence than you would expect -laid off after busy season -so much work just sent off to offshore teams it's lazy and they often don't have the accounting concept...

eh not really we live in a fallen world jesus loves you and died on a cross to have a relationship with you faith in this gives you his forgiveness no merit of your own and he is life jobs are great but he is peace life and freedom these do not reflect the views of pwc they are my own cuz they made me write something here so why not share the truth and secret to a fulfilling life
excellent place to learn lots of opportunity to grow
didn't learn that much prioritize happy hours over investing in training young staff
promotions and movement is all boys club if you can't manage upward you will fail like any other corporate ladder but here so more than others

as an audit associate at pwc i experienced one of the most grueling work environments of my career the busy season is relentless you re expected to completely sacrifice your personal life for nearly four months out of the year late nights until 2 3 am are not uncommon and weekends are often treated as regular workdays what made the experience worse was the lack of support from senior team members and management rath...
long hours no work life balance
bad hours no work life balance
- local office was one of the most toxic environments - favoritism and backstabbing - toxic partners go through high performers without any repercussion
getting burned by corporate politics as an associate
toxic at times for the employee

there is always a scandal somewhere in pwc just look at what happened in australia partners are self-serving and lacking in basic ethics internal processes are badly thought out and inefficient plenty of favoritism and bullying big words about diversity but only in some places benefits are poor no flexibility when things go wrong
1 favoritism 2 super competitive throw under the bus culture 3 performance determination is not transparent not much job security

work-life balance can be very challenging especially during busy season long hours weekends tight deadlines staff turnover is high leading to increased workload on those who stay compensation may not feel competitive compared to workload especially at the junior and senior associate levels bureaucracy and rigid processes can sometimes slow down efficiency client-facing pressure can be stressful particularly for less...
long hours and pay is not that good
sometimes talents and skills are not the only thing needed for career growth

only difference between normal consulting firm to big 4 is they are always in rush quantity over quality people often glorify overworking even when it's unnecessary simply because it gives them a thrill communicating with directors and managing directors is really challenging specially indian bosses they don t even give binary responses in contrast higher management partners and above are at least responsive and tra...
promotion is not promised and
evaluate levels of all employees to make sure they are not underutilized
don't have cons at all
bad worklife-balance and not super well compensated
wlb is not the best
long hour very impressive team does not help each other
